perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unau- thorized use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that it may consider reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unau- thorized use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient' and weakening 'shall take' to 'may consider' creates contradiction and ambiguity. Section 2 defines the 'Receiving Party' as having strict obligations, yet here 'Recipient' (intended to be the same entity) has a discretionary obligation. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement because of the weakening of the protection of confidential information and contradiction in different use of terms that reffers to the same entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information means any information disclosed to by one party to the other, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally or by inspection of tangible or intangible objects, including without limitation documents, business plans, source code, software, documentation, financial analysis, marketing plans, customer names, customer list, customer data. Confidential Information may also include information disclosed to a party by third parties at the direction of a Disclosing Party. Confidential Information shall not, however, include any information which the Receiving party can establish (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure through no action or inaction of Receiving Party; or (iii) is in the possession of Receiving Party, without confidentiality restrictions, at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party as shown by Receiving Party’s files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure. The party disclosing the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Disclosing Party” in the Agreement and the party receiving the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Receiving Party” in the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Recipient' and 'shall take' to 'may consider' creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text defines 'Receiving Party' as having strict obligations regarding confidential information. The changed text weakens this obligation by using 'Recipient' (intended to be the same entity) and making the measures discretionary ('may consider'). This introduces ambiguity and weakens the protection of confidential information, directly contradicting the original intent and defined responsibilities of the 'Receiving Party'.

perturbation.original_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of Receiving Party shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the Acquiring Party shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party and shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon the Disclosing Party’s request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Acquiring Party' creates contradiction and confusion. The agreement never defines 'Acquiring Party', leaving uncertainty on who must return documents. This contrasts with previous definitions which refer to the 'Receiving Party', introducing conflict on the obligations of document return upon request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The party disclosing the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Disclosing Party” in the Agreement and the party receiving the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Receiving Party” in the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Receiving Party', which is a defined term in the contract as per the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Acquiring Party', which is not defined anywhere in the document, introducing confusion and ambiguity. This change directly contradicts the original agreement's definition of who is responsible for returning the confidential information as the 'Acquiring Party' is undefined.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall survive for a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall survive indefinitely from the date of disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' and setting the duration to 'indefinitely' creates an contradiction. The term Proprietary Information is never previously defined in the contract, and thus, creates ambiguity. Furthermore, changing the duration to 'indefinitely' contradicts the originally defined duration of '3 years', because in the first paragraph, it reffers to the rest of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information means any information disclosed to by one party to the other, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally or by inspection of tangible or intangible objects, including without limitation documents, business plans, source code, software, documentation, financial analysis, marketing plans, customer names, customer list, customer data. Confidential Information may also include information disclosed to a party by third parties at the direction of a Disclosing Party. Confidential Information shall not, however, include any information which the Receiving party can establish (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure through no action or inaction of Receiving Party; or (iii) is in the possession of Receiving Party, without confidentiality restrictions, at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party as shown by Receiving Party’s files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information', a term not previously defined in the contract, which contradicts the section defining 'Confidential Information'. Furthermore, the change from '3 years' to 'indefinitely' directly conflicts with the contract's established duration, thus creating ambiguity and inconsistency in the agreement's terms and enforceability, creating a Misaligned Terminology In Text Contradiction. The changed duration also creates an inconsistency with the original agreement duration.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that any violation or threatened violation of this Agreement will cause irreparable injury to the Dis- closing Party, entitling the Disclosing Party to obtain injunctive relief in addition to all legal remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges that any breach or potential breach of this Understanding may cause significant harm to the Sharing Party, justifying equitable remedies along with all other legal avenues.
perturbation.explanation: Multiple term changes create an in-text contradiction. 'Receiving Party' becomes 'Recipient', 'violation' becomes 'breach', 'threatened violation' becomes 'potential breach', 'Agreement' becomes 'Understanding', 'irreparable injury' becomes 'significant harm', 'Disclosing Party' becomes 'Sharing Party', and 'injunctive relief' becomes 'equitable remedies'. This leads to ambiguity. For instance, the contract defined 'Disclosing Party' and the 'Receiving Party' in section 2. It's undefined if the obligations for 'Recipient' and 'Sharing Party' are same. These multiple term switches makes enforcement confusing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The party disclosing the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Disclosing Party” in the Agreement and the party receiving the Confidential Information shall be referred to as “Receiving Party” in the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces multiple term changes ('Receiving Party' to 'Recipient', 'violation' to 'breach', 'Agreement' to 'Understanding', 'Disclosing Party' to 'Sharing Party') that directly contradict the definitions provided in the 'Contradicted Text', which explicitly defines 'Disclosing Party' and 'Receiving Party' within the context of 'the Agreement'. These changes create ambiguity and directly conflict with the established definitions, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations and making enforcement confusing.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, without reference to conflict of laws principles. This docu-ment contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Understanding shall be binding on the participants and their respective successors and assigns. This Understanding shall be interpreted in accordance with the principles of fairness and equity. This document constitutes the complete understanding among the participants regarding the matter at hand. Failure to enforce any term of this Understanding does not waive that term or any other term.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Understanding', 'parties' to 'participants', 'governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts' to 'interpreted in accordance with the principles of fairness and equity', 'entire agreement' to 'complete understanding', and 'provision' to 'term' creates a contradiction. The initial paragraph states that the NDA, which is the 'Agreement', falls under legal jurisdiction of Massachusetts law. The change now states that the 'Understanding' is governed by principles of 'fairness and equity'. Since the document refers to both Agreement and Understanding, it is unclear under which rules any disputes should be resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIS NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“Agreement”) is made and entered into as of between thoughtbot, inc. having its place of business at 41 Winter Street, 7th Floor, Boston, MA 02108 and , having its place of business at .
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both clearly refer to the document as an 'Agreement' governed by Massachusetts law. The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Understanding' and changes the governing law to 'principles of fairness and equity', creating a contradiction because the document now ambiguously refers to itself as both an Agreement under Massachusetts law and an Understanding under principles of fairness and equity. This creates a clear contradiction, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology. It directly conflicts with the contradicted section by introducing uncertainty regarding the document's nature and governing laws.
